title: Anti-Gravity 2026: High-Leverage Workflows
audience: LinkedIn builders, engineering leaders, and AI operators

experiments.notes: Test concise workflow-led framing with chart-backed adoption close.

slides.headline: Anti-Gravity 2026: __Beyond AI Coding__
slides.subtitle: Where agent orchestration compounds
slides.accentText: MCP connectors | Multi-model routing | Artifact-first delivery

slides.headline: Why teams are paying attention now
slides.accentText: From assistant chat to execution systems
bullets: Agent mode can plan and execute multi-step tasks
MCP links live tools and data into the same flow
Artifacts keep outputs reviewable before shipping

slides.headline: __Leverage Stack__ for Serious Builds
bullets: One control layer coordinates specialist agents
Live connectors replace copy-paste context
Artifact reviews catch drift before release

cards.title: Control Plane
cards.detail: Manage plan, execution, and review in one thread.

cards.title: Context Fabric
cards.detail: Attach real docs, repos, and data via MCP.

cards.title: Orchestration Cadence
cards.detail: Time-box planning loops across specialist agents.

slides.headline: Workflow 1: __Data-aware loop__
slides.introText: Build features against **real schema and metric context**
bullets: Inspect tables and relationships before coding
Generate and run SQL inside the same session
Validate outputs against semantic definitions

slides.headline: Workflow 2: __Voice agent factory__
slides.introText: Split voice delivery into **parallel specialist lanes**
bullets: Lane A owns telephony and webhook contracts
Lane B implements STT, routing, and tool calls
Lane C validates browser and API behavior

cards.title: Runtime Lane
cards.detail: Implement speech flow and orchestration logic.

slides.headline: Workflow 3: __Model extension cockpit__
slides.introText: Route tasks to the **best-fit model**, not one model for all
bullets: Use planning models for architecture decisions
Use coding models for fast implementation passes
Keep one mission-control thread for final review

cards.title: Extension Bridge
cards.detail: Use specialist tools without splitting context.

cards.title: Unified Review
cards.detail: Approve outputs through one release gate.

slides.headline: Workflow 4: __Parallel SaaS shipping__
slides.introText: Run auth, billing, and frontend tracks **at the same time**
bullets: Track 1 ships auth and data schema changes
Track 2 ships billing APIs and payment flows
Track 3 ships UI and end-to-end checks

cards.title: Revenue Track
cards.detail: Ship billing logic and reconciliation checks.

cards.title: Experience Track
cards.detail: Deliver UI quality and E2E confidence.

slides.headline: __MCP hardening__ in practice
slides.introText: Treat connector sprawl as infrastructure, not setup noise
bullets: Standardize connector bootstrap per repo
Set explicit env and absolute path conventions
Monitor reconnect failures with local alerts

slides.headline: Week-one __adoption path__
slides.introText: Roll out in phases so teams gain confidence quickly
bullets: Phase 1: single-agent tasks plus artifact review
Phase 2: one MCP connector tied to delivery
Phase 3: role-based multi-agent orchestration

chart.title: Execution Maturity by Phase
chart.footnote: Illustrative maturity index for rollout sequencing.

slides.headline: Ready for __Anti-Gravity__ ops?
slides.ctaText: Comment AG and I will share the rollout blueprint

caption:
Anti-Gravity becomes valuable when you treat it as an execution system, not just a coding assistant.

This deck shows high-leverage workflows teams can run now:
- Data-aware engineering loops
- Voice agent factory lanes
- Multi-model extension cockpit
- Parallel SaaS shipping
- MCP reliability hardening
- Week-one adoption path

If you want the rollout blueprint, comment AG.

@maazsiddiqui
